narration: Your employer auto-enrolled you in your 401k at 3%. Sounds reasonable — except that one default is quietly costing you three hundred and forty-two thousand dollars.
visual: 401k enrollment form slides up from bottom with '3%' highlighted in amber. Counter ticks up from $0 to $342,000 below the form.

narration: Here's the wild part — 68% of auto-enrolled workers never change that default rate. They just trusted HR set it correctly. They didn't.
visual: Pie chart springs in: 68% amber slice labeled 'NEVER CHANGED', 32% green slice labeled 'CHANGED IT'. Legend fades in below.

narration: On a $55,000 salary, 3% means you're saving just $138 a month. The recommended 15% is $687. That $549 gap, compounded over time, is where your retirement disappears.
visual: Two side-by-side cards spring in: amber card shows $138/month at 3%, green card shows $687/month at 15%. Red badge shows $549/MONTH GAP.

narration: Run the math over 30 years at 7% returns — 3% gives you $170,000. Fifteen percent grows to $512,000. The only difference is a number you never changed.
visual: Two bar charts grow upward: amber bar reaches $170K, green bar reaches $512K. Callout badge shows $342,000 DIFFERENCE.

narration: Your employer picked 3% because it meets the legal minimum — not because it funds your retirement. Status quo bias does the rest — you never change it.
visual: Person silhouette at HR desk with a document stamped 'CONTRIBUTION RATE: 3%'. Amber badge: 'MINIMUM = LOW PAPERWORK'.

narration: Log into your 401k right now. If you see 3%, change it. Even bumping to 10% puts $200,000 back in your future. Follow for the move that takes two minutes.
visual: Phone shows 401k app, contribution rate animates from 3% to 10% with progress bar. Green badge: '$200,000 BACK IN YOUR FUTURE'. CTA banner fades in.
